methods.name: Stage-Progression
methods.rationale: Applies typical Seed → next-stage step-up multiplier (2.5x) to $5M total raised proxy.

methods.name: Peer-Median
methods.skip_reason: insufficient narrow peer set in Robotics & Manufacturing (found 0 same-stage, 1 adjacent-stage, 0 similar-tier; need ≥3)
methods.rationale: 12 same-sector peers exist in Robotics & Manufacturing, but none of the narrow filters (same-stage / adjacent-stage / similar-valuation-tier) yielded ≥3 datapoints. Falling back to all-sector median would be misleading.

private.name: Foundation Robotics
private.rationale: Same sector (Robotics & Manufacturing), post-money $3B+ (reported, raise in progress)

data.narrative:
Rangeview is a seed-stage robotics and manufacturing company founded by Cameron Schiller and Aeden Gasser-Brennan. The company has raised $5M+ to date. The relevant peer universe comprises 12 companies in Robotics & Manufacturing, including 8 private firms (1X Technologies, Foundation Robotics, Divergent, Exotec, and Locus Robotics among them) and 1 public comparable.

A peer-median approach proved inapplicable due to insufficient narrow-set comparables; no seed-stage or adjacent-stage peers within similar valuation tiers were available in sufficient density to support triangulation. Consequently, stage-progression analysis provides the primary valuation signal. Using total raised ($5M) as a base value proxy and applying a typical 2.5x step-up multiplier from seed to next stage yields an implied valuation of $12M. This reflects conventional early-stage progression mechanics absent company-specific performance data.

The analysis supports a valuation range of $11M to $14M, with a median point of $12M. This range is anchored to a single methodological input and carries material uncertainty. Key limitations include the sparse comparable set within Rangeview's narrow stage-and-sector band, absence of revenue, customer, or product-stage data, and reliance on a mechanical stage multiplier. Resolution of valuation would benefit from additional seed-stage robotics comparables and clarity on Rangeview's development progress relative to peers.
